Why Fed Cuts Beat Rate Hikes
items.summary: Markets may be pricing yesterday's economy. iCapital's Dan Suzuki joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why slowing job growth and cooling inflation make Fed rate cuts more likely than hikes, why higher Treasury yields haven't broken the stock rally, and why AI leadership may be shifting away from chipmakers toward hyperscalers that can actually monetize their massive investments. (Source: Blo

items.title: Tech Drives US Stocks Higher as Traders Downplay Iran War Risks
items.summary: US stocks rose as investors put aside concerns over the latest flare-up between the US and Iran to pile on technology stocks after recent declines spurred dip buying.

items.title: Brazil Miner Vale Facing Scrutiny Over Chair’s Exit Compensation
items.summary: Vale SA, the world’s top iron ore producer, is under scrutiny by Brazil’s securities regulator following media reports that this week’s resignation of its board chair included a deal for a compensation package.

items.title: Final Stage of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s Funeral Begins After Delay
items.summary: Thousands gathered for the procession in Mashhad for the supreme leader, who was killed in the first U.S.-Israeli attacks of the war. Hours earlier, the United States and Iran traded strikes.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: US strikes Iran Chabahar port for first time as conflict expands beyond Strait of Hormuz | World News
US Missile Stockpiles Under Pressure
items.summary: Germany is buying US Tomahawk missiles even as Europe races to build its own defense industry. Bloomberg Economics' Becca Wasser joins Open Interest to explain why America is prioritizing its own missile stockpiles, why Europe still depends on U.S. weapons, and how China's industrial strength is reshaping the global military balance. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Flexport CEO Warns Shipping Risks Remain
items.summary: Flexport CEO Ryan Petersen joins Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why the biggest threat to global shipping isn't the Strait of Hormuz—it's still the Red Sea. He breaks down the surge in imports driven by tariff uncertainty, why companies are stockpiling inventory after the COVID supply chain crisis, and how Panama Canal drought risks could create the next bottleneck. (Source: Bloomberg)

EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.

items.title: Oil prices fall more than 2% as mediators try to prevent U.S. and Iran from returning to war
items.summary: Oil prices turned lower on Thursday after the U.S. carried out fresh strikes on Iran, renewing concerns about supply disruptions in the Middle East.

items.title: Ukrainians Cautiously Welcome Trump’s Announcement About Patriot Missile Systems
items.summary: President Trump said the United States would license Ukraine to produce Patriots, which can intercept ballistic missiles. But it could be months or years before those are ready.

items.title: ING Weighs Venezuela Commodity Deals as Client Requests Rise
items.summary: ING Groep, one of the top financiers of commodity trading, is fielding “a lot” of calls from clients to support Venezuelan natural resource deals as the country revamps exports after years of US sanctions, its new Americas commodity finance head said.
